titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on dental care subsidies.
html:
In this document, the government presents its assessment of the observations and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in the report "Dental Care Subsidies – A System with Flaws" (RiR 2025:34).

The Swedish National Audit Office's overall conclusion is that the dental care subsidy system is not effective. The Swedish National Audit Office makes four recommendations to the government, as well as three to the Dental and Pharmaceutical Benefits Agency and one to the Consumer Agency.

The government welcomes the Swedish National Audit Office's review and notes that it highlights the need to develop the state-funded dental care system to make it more effective. Given the extensive measures that the government has already taken, which largely align with the review's analysis and proposals, and with the intention of taking further action in the areas identified by the Swedish National Audit Office, the government agrees only partially with the Swedish National Audit Office's observations and recommendations.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The government has responded to a report indicating that the current system of dental care subsidies is not functioning as effectively as it should. They aim to improve the system to ensure that funds are used appropriately.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors whether the state uses its money effectively. They have conducted an audit of the state's dental care subsidies and concluded that the system is not efficient. This means that the support is not reaching those who need it, or is not functioning as intended.

In its report, the National Audit Office proposes changes to the government and other agencies, such as the Dental and Pharmaceutical Benefits Agency and the Consumer Agency. They want the rules surrounding dental care to be changed to improve the system.

The government states that they have already implemented many changes that are similar to the proposals put forward by the National Audit Office. They believe that the audit is valuable because it demonstrates the need for further development of dental care support.

The government agrees with some aspects of the criticism but not with everything. Now, the government believes that they have responded fully to the report and that the matter is closed.
